_meta.note: Synthetic Slack export. Two kinds of threads: live incident chatter and historical Q&A. Each thread id IS its source_id.

messages.text: checkout error rate is spiking — 40% 5xx on checkout-svc, started right as the v4.2.0 deploy began.
DB dashboard shows lock waits piling up on the orders table. Writes are blocked.
The deploy ran the order-tagging migration. Rolling back v4.2.0 now.
Rollback done, error rate recovering. Release is delayed. Opening a postmortem (NW-1098).
Ack. v4.2.0 slips to later this week once we understand the migration issue.
payments p99 is at 8s. No error spike, just slow. Started this afternoon.
It's the new fraud-check call from NW-1120. It's synchronous and has no timeout, and fraud-check is slow under peak load.
Classic hot-path violation — we blocked auth on a non-critical service. Needs a timeout + fallback or go async.
Opening NW-1130. Will make it async with a 150ms timeout.
Are we settled on the orders DB? Someone asked why not DynamoDB.
Postgres. We need real multi-item transactions (order + items + payment link) and relational reporting. Writing it up as ADR-007.
+1, and both teams already run Postgres. DynamoDB's transaction limits don't fit our pattern.
Just NW-1148 — the flaky checkout-svc integration tests. We won't tag an RC until CI is green. Nothing else is blocking.
Who do I ping about a stuck inventory sync?
Me — I own inventory-svc. Stuck syncs are almost always consumer lag on the stock-updates Kafka topic.
